intro: Diogoa zenkeri, also known as the "African dog-bone tree," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa, characterized by its unique, finger-like roots that resemble dog bones. Diogoa zenkeri has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Diogoa zenkeri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Diogoa zenkeri include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and fever.
